description: This file defines yes–no question formation in the Future Continuous tense in Njo Viri (Balanda). Future continuous questions ask whether an action will be ongoing at a future time.

general_principle.structure: Future continuous questions in Balanda are formed by keeping the future continuous structure unchanged and adding the question marker 'we?' at the end of the sentence.

question_structure.structure: Affirmative or Negative Future Continuous sentence + we?
question_structure.notes: The future marker 'le-' must remain attached to 'dho'; The auxiliary 'dho' must be present; The progressive prefix 'ti-' must be present; Word order must not change; The question marker 'we?' must be sentence-final

affirmative_questions.notes: Ne ledho timande we?; Danda ledho titokpenze neu we?

negative_questions.structure: Subject + le-dho + ti-Verb + (Object / Complement) + gba + we?
negative_questions.notes: Ne ledho timande gba we?; Danda ledho titokpenze neu gba we?

tense_and_aspect_distinction.structure: Future continuous questions encode both future tense and progressive aspect.
tense_and_aspect_distinction.notes: 'le-' encodes future tense; 'dho' functions as auxiliary 'be'; 'ti-' encodes progressive aspect

error_constraints.structure: Do not omit 'le-'; Do not omit 'dho'; Do not omit 'ti-'; Do not use the copula 'ma'; Do not use the result marker 'dheki'; Do not move 'gba' or 'we?' from their fixed positions

model_behavior_guidelines.structure: {"enforce_le_dho_auxiliary": true, "enforce_ti_prefix": true, "preserve_word_order": true, "keep_we_sentence_final": true, "allow_affirmative_and_negative_questions": true, "map_to_english_future_continuous_questions": true}

output: Will I be eating fish?
Will she be drinking water?

input: Danda ledho titeli muru famvara we?
output: Will we be sleeping on the bed?
Will Uku be going?
Will Ngande be coming home?

input: Ndi ledho tinonö gbi difo we?
output: Will they be walking in the compound?
Will she be running fast?

input: Uku ledho timande gbi njü we?
output: Will Uku be working in the garden?

input: Wiki ledho timoliki gbi difo we?
output: Will the child be playing in the compound?
Will I be watching the dog?

input: Ne ledho tivele njo balanda we?
output: Will I be speaking Balanda?
Will the girl be writing words?
Will I be reading a book?
Will he be thinking about it?
Will they be knowing it?
Will I be asking them?

input: Ngande ledho tivele zi ndi we?
output: Will Ngande be telling them?

input: Wilii ledho tikeye ti naa ni we?
output: Will the girl be calling her mother?
Will I be loving you?

input: Ni ledho timaguku ti kpörö we?
output: Will he be fearing the snake?
Will she be hating him?
Will Ngande be surprising him?
Will he be seeming calm?
Will the girl be tying the shoe?
Will he be cutting the pot?

Will the girl be washing the shoe?
Will I be hiding the beads?
Will Ngande be building a house?

Will she be changing the words?
Will I be turning the door?
Will we be adding money?
Will I be describing Balanda?

Will Ngande be initiating the project?
Will I be contributing to the project?
Will the girl be separating trash?
Will we be upholding the law?
Will I be wanting water?
Will she be giving money?

Will Uku be trying the cloth?
Will Ngande be leaving home?
Will I be arriving in Wau?
Will he be sharpening the knife?
Will Ngande be imitating adults?
Will we be starting the project?
Will he be falling?
